deep_dive.title: Automated sleep scoring in hibernating and non-hibernating American black bears
deep_dive.abstract: Hibernating bears show remarkable metabolic suppression. Their decline in core body temperature (Tb) is moderate(from 38{degrees}C to 30-35{degrees}C), but their metabolism declines as much as 75%. To understand the role of sleep in this hypometabolic state, we recorded biotelemetrically EEG, EOG and EMG data over 3500 days from 16 captive American black bears in and out of hibernation under semi-natural conditions. This data set is too large to score manually for Wake, REM- and NREM sleep, so we tested two machine learning classifiers: (1) Somnotate trained on multiple one-day recordings, and (2) Somnivore, trained on a small subset from each recording. As automated scoring methods have not been applied to hibernating species before, a major concern is the effect changing brain temperature has on the EEG and on the machine learning based detection. Therefore, we selected reference data using consensus by 3 manual sleep scorers from each of 6 bears, two one-day recordings at the highest and lowest body temperatures during hibernation when Tb was oscillating in multiday cycles, and a non-hibernating one-day recording in summer. Somnotate results were excellent when trained separately for hibernating and non-hibernating data. Training Somnotate separately for high and low Tbb within hibernation did not improve results further. Sleep times in hibernation were about 2x that in summer for both automated scores and manual scores (p<0.0001). There were no significant differences in occupancy of vigilance states between automated and manual scores in hibernation (p>0.05), but a small overestimate of sleep time in summer (p<0.05). Both applications yielded F-measures against manual scores in the 0.90-0.98 range. Outliers in the 0.67-0.88 range were correlated between the two applications, indicating that specific files are more challenging to annotate. We conclude that both applications have accuracies approaching that of manual scorers when trained on high quality data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hibernating bears metabolic suppression and sleep scoring
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 冬眠熊代谢抑制与睡眠评分
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 冬眠熊代谢抑制与睡眠评分
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 冬眠熊代谢抑制与睡眠评分
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies sleep in hibernating bears, recording EEG/EOG/EMG and testing ML classifiers for automated sleep scoring, linking metabolic suppression to sleep states.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了冬眠熊的睡眠，通过生物遥测记录EEG、EOG和EMG数据，并测试了两种机器学习分类器（Somnotate和Somnivore）进行自动睡眠评分。研究发现冬眠熊代谢抑制高达75%，体温中度下降。该方法首次应用于冬眠物种，有助于理解睡眠在低代谢状态中的作用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文研究了冬眠熊的睡眠，通过生物遥测记录EEG、EOG和EMG数据，并测试了两种机器学习分类器（Somnotate和Somnivore）进行自动睡眠评分。研究发现冬眠熊代谢抑制高达75%，体温中度下降。该方法首次应用于冬眠物种，有助于理解睡眠在低代谢状态中的作用。

deep_dive.title: Sleep to forget: active control of consolidation and forgetting by slow-wave sleep dynamics
deep_dive.abstract: Sleep supports both the consolidation of new memories and the forgetting of others, but how the cortex flexibly controls these outcomes remains poorly understood. Recent work has shown that two types of Up states may play distinct, competing roles during slow-wave sleep (SWS): slow waves actively consolidate memory traces, whereas delta waves promote their weakening. Here we use a biophysical thalamocortical network model equipped with spike-timing-dependent plasticity to investigate the synaptic mechanisms underlying this dissociation. By manipulating the intrinsic Ca2+ dynamics of cortical pyramidal cells, we generate both slow and delta wave Up states within a single network. Using a sequence-learning task paradigm we recapitulate the optogenetic dissociation: removing plasticity during slow waves degrades the memory, while removing it during delta waves enhances consolidation. Mechanistically, the model reveals the longer slow wave Up state affords a spontaneous reactivation phase, occurring after the interfering input, during which the trained memory is selectively reactivated and protected, a phase the truncated delta Up state cannot support. We further find that delta waves sparsen the synaptic representation more than slow waves and predict that the balance between consolidation and forgetting can be flexibly tuned by the ratio of slow to delta waves during SWS.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: focuses on slow-wave sleep dynamics controlling memory consolidation and forgetting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 聚焦慢波睡眠动态控制记忆巩固与遗忘
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 聚焦慢波睡眠动态控制记忆巩固与遗忘
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 聚焦慢波睡眠动态控制记忆巩固与遗忘
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses a biophysical thalamocortical network model with STDP to show that slow-wave Up states consolidate memories while delta-wave Up states weaken them, providing a mechanistic basis for active forgetting during sleep.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 睡眠中记忆巩固与遗忘的灵活控制机制尚不清楚。本研究构建生物物理丘脑皮层网络模型，利用尖峰时序可塑性，通过操纵锥体细胞钙动力学同时产生慢波和delta波Up状态。在序列学习任务中，发现慢波Up状态巩固记忆痕迹，而delta波Up状态则削弱记忆，揭示了皮层通过不同Up状态竞争调控记忆的机制，为睡眠中主动遗忘提供了神经计算基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 睡眠中记忆巩固与遗忘的灵活控制机制尚不清楚。本研究构建生物物理丘脑皮层网络模型，利用尖峰时序可塑性，通过操纵锥体细胞钙动力学同时产生慢波和delta波Up状态。在序列学习任务中，发现慢波Up状态巩固记忆痕迹，而delta波Up状态则削弱记忆，揭示了皮层通过不同Up状态竞争调控记忆的机制，为睡眠中主动遗忘提供了神经计算基础。

deep_dive.title: Sleep enhances spatial schema memory formation in humans
deep_dive.abstract: Schema memory, a generalized representation formed across episodes sharing regularities, is thought to arise through sleep-dependent systems consolidation. Yet, direct evidence in humans remains sparse. Here, sixty young adults navigated a virtual-reality arena and learned a spatial distribution of object-category ratios across five sessions (toys vs. household items, hidden in boxes at different locations). Participants then either slept or were sleep-deprived for a full night, followed by two recovery nights before memory testing, or they were tested after a short 30-min delay spent awake. Only after a three-day delay did spatial memory of old box locations predict spatial interpolation to new box locations, indicating time-dependent schema expression. Critically, sleep distinctly enhanced spatial integration beyond the effect of time. This benefit was predicted by frontal cortical slow oscillation-spindle coupling during the first post-encoding night, thus linking sleep oscillations to the transformation of episodic spatial memories into integrated schema representations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Sleep enhances spatial schema memory formation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 睡眠增强空间图式记忆形成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 睡眠增强空间图式记忆形成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 睡眠增强空间图式记忆形成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that sleep-dependent consolidation is crucial for forming spatial schema memory in humans, with expression only after three days including sleep, supporting systems consolidation theory.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究通过虚拟现实导航任务发现，睡眠对于空间图式记忆的形成至关重要。60名年轻人在学习物体类别空间分布后，经过整夜睡眠或睡眠剥夺，仅在三天延迟后（含有睡眠）才表现出对新位置的空间插值能力，证明时间依赖的图式表达需要睡眠。结果直接支持了睡眠依赖的系统巩固假说，揭示了睡眠在高级认知中的关键作用，是睡眠神经科学领域的重要进展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究通过虚拟现实导航任务发现，睡眠对于空间图式记忆的形成至关重要。60名年轻人在学习物体类别空间分布后，经过整夜睡眠或睡眠剥夺，仅在三天延迟后（含有睡眠）才表现出对新位置的空间插值能力，证明时间依赖的图式表达需要睡眠。结果直接支持了睡眠依赖的系统巩固假说，揭示了睡眠在高级认知中的关键作用，是睡眠神经科学领域的重要进展。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 研究睡眠神经科学领域的学术文章, especially work that connects or combines: advancements in sleep neuroscience research; neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness; neural regulation of the sleep-wake cycle; brain circuits involved in sleep control; neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep; neurobiology of non-rapid eye movement sleep; recent advances in sleep neuroscience research; neural mechanisms underlying sleep wake regulation; neurobiological basis of clinical sleep disorders.

deep_dive.title: Better Adherence, Richer Context: A Field Evaluation of LLM-Powered Conversational Voice Diaries for Sleep
deep_dive.abstract: Sleep diaries are central to behavioral sleep medicine and cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia, yet daily completion is difficult to sustain, and static forms often provide limited context for interpreting night-to-night sleep variation. We designed an LLM-powered conversational voice diary that delivers clinically grounded morning and evening sleep diary questions through proactive smart-speaker prompts, structured conversational intake, and adaptive follow-up dialogue. We evaluated the system in a four-week between-subjects field study with 30 university students, comparing it with a text-based mobile diary using matched diary items, reporting windows, and reminder intervals. Compared with the text-based diary, the conversational voice diary showed higher adherence and elicited more detailed contextual self-report about routines, stressors, environmental conditions, and other sleep-related factors. Participants also described the voice diary as easier to integrate into daily routines, despite longer perceived completion time. However, voice-based conversational intake produced lower completeness for some structured diary fields, revealing a trade-off between expressive richness and structured precision. These findings show both the promise and the challenge of using LLM-powered conversational voice assistants for longitudinal health self-report.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates an LLM-powered conversational voice diary for sleep monitoring, directly advancing sleep research methods
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估基于LLM的对话式语音睡眠日记，直接推进睡眠研究方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估基于LLM的对话式语音睡眠日记，直接推进睡眠研究方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估基于LLM的对话式语音睡眠日记，直接推进睡眠研究方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This field study compares an LLM-powered conversational voice diary with a text-based mobile diary for sleep monitoring, showing better adherence and richer context for sleep variation interpretation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究设计了一个基于大型语言模型的对话式语音日记系统，通过智能音箱主动提问和自适应对话收集睡眠数据。在30名大学生中进行了为期四周的田野实验，与文本日记对比。结果显示，语音日记显著提高了依从性，并提供了更丰富的夜间睡眠变异背景信息。这项工作为睡眠医学中的行为干预和临床评估提供了创新工具，直接推动了睡眠神经科学领域的方法学进步。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究设计了一个基于大型语言模型的对话式语音日记系统，通过智能音箱主动提问和自适应对话收集睡眠数据。在30名大学生中进行了为期四周的田野实验，与文本日记对比。结果显示，语音日记显著提高了依从性，并提供了更丰富的夜间睡眠变异背景信息。这项工作为睡眠医学中的行为干预和临床评估提供了创新工具，直接推动了睡眠神经科学领域的方法学进步。

quick_skim.title: Quantum machine learning for detection of sleep deprivation from EEG signals
quick_skim.abstract:
Approximately 50% of the population in India is estimated to experience sleep-related disorders. Sleep deprivation is a prevalent condition that adversely impacts cognitive performance, neural functioning, and overall health. Electroencephalography (EEG) offers an objective means of capturing neural alterations associated with sleep loss, making it well-suited for automated detection frameworks. In this study, we explore the application of a Quantum Support Vector Machine and Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks to classify sleep-deprived and well-rested states using resting-state EEG signals.

A comprehensive feature extraction pipeline is employed, incorporating spectral band power, band ratios, Hjorth parameters, and functional connectivity measures. These features are subsequently encoded into quantum states to construct a quantum kernel, which is then utilized for classification. Model performance is evaluated under both epoch-level and subject-level data partitioning schemes.

The Hybrid Quantum Neural Network (HQNN) achieves the highest performance across both evaluation settings, attaining an accuracy of 96.88% at the epoch level and 81.25% at the subject level. The QSVM model achieves accuracies of 93.75% and 75.00% for epoch-level and subject-level evaluations, respectively. At subject-level and epoch -level evaluation, HQNN outperforms previously reported results (68.23% and 95.72%). Overall, these findings highlight the potential of quantum machine learning as a competitive approach for EEG-based sleep deprivation detection, with promising implications for real-world biomedical applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: detects sleep deprivation from EEG signals using quantum machine learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用量子机器学习从脑电图信号检测睡眠剥夺
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用量子机器学习从脑电图信号检测睡眠剥夺
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用量子机器学习从脑电图信号检测睡眠剥夺
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study applies quantum support vector machine and hybrid quantum neural networks to classify sleep-deprived vs well-rested states from resting-state EEG, extracting spectral and connectivity features.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本研究针对睡眠剥夺的自动检测问题，提出使用量子支持向量机和混合量子神经网络对静息态脑电图信号进行分类。通过提取频谱功率、频带比率、Hjorth参数和功能连接等特征，模型有效区分了睡眠剥夺与休息良好的状态。该工作展示了量子机器学习在睡眠神经科学中的潜力，为临床睡眠障碍的非侵入性检测提供了新方法。

quick_skim.title: Neuronal Activity-Dependent Electroosmosis and Its Potential Role in Interstitial Fluid Flow in the Glymphatic System
quick_skim.abstract: The mechanisms driving interstitial fluid flow through brain parenchyma remain unresolved, limiting our understanding of how fluid transport contributes to glymphatic waste clearance and broader aspects of brain metabolism and neuronal activity. Existing theories based on diffusion or pressure gradients fail to explain sustained, directional flow through the tortuous extracellular space (ECS), particularly under normal physiologic conditions. Here we propose that electroosmosis, fluid motion driven by endogenous electric fields acting on charged brain tissue, provides a biophysically consistent mechanism for intraparenchymal interstitial fluid transport while generating pressure distributions favorable for periarterial influx and perivenous efflux. Using computational modeling informed by anatomical reconstructions of ECS microstructure and local field potential (LFP) recordings, we show that electroosmotic flow generates physiologically realistic velocities and reproduces brain state dependent differences in glymphatic transport, including the enhanced glymphatic flow observed during sleep compared to wakefulness. A physics-informed reduced-order model (ROM) further demonstrates that these microstructure-resolved results upscale consistently to tissue-level transport. Moreover, electroosmotic flow can induce directional pressure gradients across perivascular interfaces, facilitating both influx and efflux. This mechanism provides a unifying framework linking neuronal activity, parenchymal flow, and compartmental pressure regulation. In contrast, pressure gradients substantially larger than physiological estimates generated much smaller velocities and failed to account for the observed transport rates. These findings address a major gap in glymphatic physiology and suggest that modulation of electric field properties, via endogenous activity or external neuromodulation, could serve as a therapeutic strategy to enhance solute clearance in neurological disorders.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: glymphatic system and sleep-related fluid dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 类淋巴系统与睡眠相关流体动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 类淋巴系统与睡眠相关流体动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 类淋巴系统与睡眠相关流体动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes electroosmosis as a biophysical mechanism for interstitial fluid flow in the brain, providing a new perspective on glymphatic waste clearance that is highly relevant to sleep neuroscience since glymphatic function is sleep-dependent.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究提出电渗作用作为脑实质间质液流动的新机制，基于内源性电场驱动带电脑组织中的液体运动。现有理论难以解释脑脊液在曲折细胞外空间中的定向流动。该机制不仅解释了脑内液体运输的物理基础，还可能与睡眠-觉醒周期中的类淋巴清除功能密切相关，为睡眠神经科学中的脑代谢和废物清除提供了新的机制性见解。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究提出电渗作用作为脑实质间质液流动的新机制，基于内源性电场驱动带电脑组织中的液体运动。现有理论难以解释脑脊液在曲折细胞外空间中的定向流动。该机制不仅解释了脑内液体运输的物理基础，还可能与睡眠-觉醒周期中的类淋巴清除功能密切相关，为睡眠神经科学中的脑代谢和废物清除提供了新的机制性见解。

quick_skim.title: Human striatal population state dynamics
quick_skim.abstract: Animal models reveal that striatal projection neurons (SPNs) fluctuate between discrete electrophysiological states with distinct levels of cortical interaction. These dynamics and their behavioral relevance remain uncharacterized in humans. Leveraging neurobiologically informed modeling of over 3 billion voxel-frame-wise striatal coactivation profiles with cortex in functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), we identified population-level striatal states in humans resembling canonical SPN states that reorganized systematically with task demands, arousal and behavior. A background of low- and moderate-coactivation "down-like" and "up-like" striatal rest states with high transition reciprocity modulated task reaction times and reward reactivity. Meanwhile, sparse, disproportionately high-magnitude  bursts of striatal coactivation with cortex, which emerged preferentially from the up-like rest state and whose cortical input composition varied with task context, tracked task engagement and arousal level. Findings bring a critical feature of corticostriatal neurobiology into systems-level view in humans and reveal a subthreshold state architecture whose balance encodes behaviorally relevant information.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies human striatal state dynamics related to arousal and behavior, overlapping with sleep-wake regulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究人类纹状体状态动态与觉醒和行为的关系，涉及睡眠-觉醒调控
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究人类纹状体状态动态与觉醒和行为的关系，涉及睡眠-觉醒调控
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究人类纹状体状态动态与觉醒和行为的关系，涉及睡眠-觉醒调控
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Using neurobiologically informed modeling of striatal-cortical coactivation from fMRI, this paper identifies human striatal population states that reorganize with task demands, arousal, and behavior, linking to SPN-like states.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 人类纹状体群体状态动态及其行为相关性尚不清楚。本研究基于fMRI中超过30亿体素-帧的纹状体-皮层共激活数据，利用神经生物学模型识别出类似SPN的群体状态。这些状态包括低共激活的“down样”和高共激活的“up样”纹状体静息状态，随任务需求、觉醒和行为系统重组，并调节反应时间和奖赏反应。揭示了纹状体状态在觉醒和行为调控中的重要作用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 人类纹状体群体状态动态及其行为相关性尚不清楚。本研究基于fMRI中超过30亿体素-帧的纹状体-皮层共激活数据，利用神经生物学模型识别出类似SPN的群体状态。这些状态包括低共激活的“down样”和高共激活的“up样”纹状体静息状态，随任务需求、觉醒和行为系统重组，并调节反应时间和奖赏反应。揭示了纹状体状态在觉醒和行为调控中的重要作用。
